name: Bunny.net API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly CDN and edge storage platform with APIs for managing CDN zones, edge storage (BunnyCDN Storage), video streaming (BunnyStream), and image optimization.
use_cases: Managing CDN pull zones and purging cache via API from agents
Storing and serving files from edge storage via REST API
Video upload and streaming management with BunnyStream
Configuring CDN rules and edge scripts via API
not_for: Teams needing advanced image transformation/manipulation (use Cloudinary/imgix)
Enterprise-grade SLAs with dedicated support contracts
best_when: You need a cost-effective CDN with a simple API for cache management, edge storage, or video streaming.
avoid_when: You need on-the-fly image transformations, or require AWS/Azure-native CDN features.

auth.notes: Account-level API key for management operations. Storage zone password for file operations. Simple header-based auth (AccessKey). Very agent-friendly.

pricing.paid_tiers: Bandwidth from $0.01/GB (Tier 1), storage from $0.02/GB/month, video from $0.0055/minute
pricing.notes: Very competitive pricing, often 3-5x cheaper than Cloudflare/AWS CloudFront. No monthly minimum. Pay-as-you-go for all services.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP status codes with basic error messages. Documentation is functional but not exceptional.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: File uploads to storage are idempotent (overwrite). Cache purge operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: No official SDK - agents must build their own HTTP client integration
Storage zones and CDN pull zones are separate concepts - agents must understand the model
Cache purge propagation can take up to 60 seconds globally
Storage API uses different base URL than management API

security_score_components.security_notes: CDN/storage API. Account API key + storage zone password. Pull zone tokens for signed URLs. Separate API keys per zone recommended for isolation.
